message:
Implement async-safe transaction drop rollback for SQLite connections

Replace synchronous ROLLBACK-on-drop with an atomic needs_rollback flag
that defers rollback to the next blocking pool operation. This prevents
the Drop impl from blocking the async executor thread while waiting for
the connection lock.

The mechanism works by:
1. SqliteTransaction::drop() sets needs_rollback via Release store
2. Every blocking-pool operation (execute, execute_batch, query_rows,
   pragma_busy_timeout) checks the flag with AcqRel swap at entry
3. If set, issues ROLLBACK before proceeding with the queued operation
4. Both SqliteConnection constructors (open and open_in_memory)
   initialize the flag to false

This ensures transaction isolation is maintained without risk of
priority inversion or executor thread stalls, while guaranteeing the
rollback executes before any subsequent statement on the same connection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
